title: AIM Curator Drift Register (aim-curator-drift-v1.0)
description: File-output schema for the deterministic Curator stage (Sprint 3 Step 4). LLM-free: no curator_model, no advisory_notes, no structured_claims. Output is a drift register emitted every cycle (including null cycles) with four typed triggers (T1-T4) firing at advisory severity only. Non-blocking by design (F5/F6). Per AMEND-G canonical-parity: if an LLM is added to Curator later, an internal LLM-output schema would sit at pipeline/monitors/ai-governance/curator-schema.json; this file-output schema remains at the public path.

input_provenance.description: Which prior cycles' chains + interpret outputs this computation consumed.

triggers_fired.description: One entry per trigger firing this cycle. Empty array if no triggers fired. Curator is non-blocking (F6): a fired trigger is advisory only, never halts the publish chain.

evidence.description: Trigger-specific evidence payload. Shape varies by trigger; see curator_base.py _trigger_T1..T4.

flags.description: Trigger-level flags, e.g. 'awaiting_appendix_a_patches' when T3 fires gated on AMEND-D closure.

cycle_summary.description: Summary stats Curator computed this cycle, regardless of whether triggers fired. Present even on null cycles so carry-forward and shape continuity are measurable.

methodology_drift_status.description: G11 (AD-2026-04-26-AJ) — deterministic methodology-vs-persistent-state drift check. Always emitted; `uninitialised: true` when persistent-state lacks _meta.required_modules; `uninitialised: false` with full live fields otherwise. Curator never raises on this field.

oneOf.description: Uninitialised case: persistent-state missing or lacks _meta.required_modules.
Live case: persistent-state declared _meta.required_modules; full comparison performed.

curator_error.description: True if the Curator itself hit an internal error (missing input, validation failure). Strictly orthogonal to triggers_fired — an error run emits {} triggers + curator_error: true.

curator_flags.description: Register-level flags (not trigger-scoped). E.g. 'window_incomplete' when fewer than window_size prior cycles exist.

schema_canonicalizations.description: Telemetry-only list of accepted schema alias canonicalisations observed while validating curator inputs. Presence does not indicate curator failure and is non-blocking.
